summary: Advanced matching for the large ftCommon file `ftCo_0A01.c`, bringing the file report to 16.76% matched for an 81.08 KB unit. The PR replaced multiple `/// #...` stubs with C for fighter/common logic including `ftCo_800A3554`, `ftCo_800A50D4`, `ftCo_800A92CC`, `ftCo_800AFC40`, `ftCo_800B0918`, and `ftCo_800B0CA8`, and tightened several function prototypes in `ftCo_0A01.h`. It also improved supporting type knowledge: `StageCameraInfo` now embeds a `StageBlastZone cam_bounds`, camera-bound users in ground/stage code were updated to `.cam_bounds.left/right/top/bottom`, `ftCo_803C6594` became `StageBlastZone**`, and `Fighter_x1A88` gained a typed `Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t` record/ring-buffer-like substructure with `cur_pos`, `facing_dir`, and link pointers.
what_changed: Added substantial C implementations in `ftCo_0A01.c` for previously stubbed or partially stubbed ftCommon functions, notably `ftCo_800A3554`, `ftCo_800A50D4`, `ftCo_800A92CC`, `ftCo_800AFC40`, `ftCo_800B0918`, and `ftCo_800B0CA8`.
Changed `ftCo_803C6594` from `UNK_T*` to `StageBlastZone**` and added `gr/types.h` includes where needed.
Changed `ftCo_800A1CC4` call sites and prototype from an untyped three-argument form using `StageCameraInfo`/data pieces to a typed `ftCo_800A1CC4(ftCo_Fighter* fp, StageBlastZone*)` form.

Typed more `ftCo_0A01.h` prototypes, replacing several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with concrete signatures such as `ftCo_800AECF0(ftCo_Fighter*)`, `ftCo_800AFC40(ftCo_Fighter*)`, `ftCo_800B0918(ftCo_Fighter* fp0, ftCo_Fighter* fp1)`, `ftCo_800B0CA8(ftCo_Fighter* fp0, ftCo_Fighter* fp1)`, `ftCo_800A50D4(ftCo_Fighter*)`, `ftCo_800A75DC(ftCo_Fighter*, ftCo_Fighter*)`, and `ftCo_800A92CC(ftCo_Fighter*)`.
Introduced `struct Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t` in `ft/types.h`, with pad/input bytes, `Vec3 cur_pos`, `float facing_dir`, a `x1C` self-link pointer, and a `STATIC_ASSERT` for size `0x348`.
Refined `struct Fighter_x1A88_t`: `x64` became a `Vec2`, the large anonymous area at `xFC` became a typed `Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t`, and `x444`/`x448` became pointers to that type.

Renamed/reshaped camera-bound storage in `StageCameraInfo`: separate fields `cam_bounds_left/right/top/bottom` were replaced by embedded `StageBlastZone cam_bounds`, and `ground.c`/`stage.c` users were updated accordingly.
Fixed existing `ftCo_800B0AF4` field usage from `data->x448->offset.x/y` to `data->x448->cur_pos.x/y`, consistent with the new typed `Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t` layout.
The PR report lists many fully matched functions already at 100%, while this slice specifically improved several lower-scoring larger functions; the file-level report is 16.76% for `ftCo_0A01.c`.
smart_moves: Reused an existing semantic type, `StageBlastZone`, for `StageCameraInfo.cam_bounds` instead of keeping four parallel float fields. This clarified offsets while reducing repeated field names and made it possible to type `ftCo_803C6594` and `ftCo_800A1CC4` more accurately.

Added a dedicated `Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t` substructure rather than leaving a large byte array plus anonymous pointer shape. This exposed the copied input state, position, facing direction, and link pointer used by `ftCo_800B0918` and `ftCo_800B0AF4`.
Kept `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t) == 0x348)` after carving out the new nested fighter structure, protecting the fragile `Fighter_x1A88` layout during future edits.
Converted prototypes in the local header before or alongside implementations, letting new matches call helpers with meaningful fighter/item/stage types instead of `UNK_PARAMS`.
Used small inline helpers (`inlineE0`, `inlineM0`, `inlineD2`, empty `inlineD3`) to influence codegen and share repeated decompiler-derived expressions without prematurely inventing high-level names.

Preserved decompiler/matching artifacts such as `M2C_FIELD`, explicit gotos, duplicate-return comments, and `PAD_STACK` where they were still needed for matching rather than over-cleaning control flow.
decomp_lessons: When four adjacent camera-bound floats have the same left/right/top/bottom shape as blast zones, embedding or reusing `StageBlastZone` can unlock cleaner field access and improve downstream function signatures.

For large fighter extension areas like `x1A88`, carve out typed islands only when the offsets are supported by multiple users; here `ftCo_800B0918` writes input/position history and `ftCo_800B0AF4` reads it back.

Changing a struct field from an anonymous `Vec2 offset` to `Vec3 cur_pos` can reveal a better semantic model and prevent misleading future names; the diff shows existing interpolation code updated to read `.cur_pos.x/y`.

Do not remove m2c scaffolding too early in hard functions. This PR keeps `M2C_FIELD`, gotos, loop labels, and comments about duplicate return nodes in functions such as `ftCo_800AFC40` and `ftCo_800B0CA8`.

Header cleanup is part of matching: typed prototypes reduce accidental argument-count/type mismatches and make call-site repairs visible, as with `ftCo_800B0E98` now passing both fighters to `ftCo_800B0CA8`.

Use concrete game enums and constants when evidence is present, such as `It_Kind_Heart`, `It_Kind_Tomato`, `It_Kind_Foods`, `It_Kind_Egg`, `GA_Ground`, and `GA_Air`, but keep numeric motion IDs where no named enum is established.
naming_conventions: Unidentified ftCommon functions retain address-based names such as `ftCo_800AFC40`, `ftCo_800B0918`, and `ftCo_800B0CA8`.
Nested fighter scratch/history structs follow offset-based names, e.g. `Fighter_x1A88_t` and newly added `Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t`.
Partially known fields preserve offset names (`x444`, `x448`, `x1C`, `x60`) while semantically supported fields use descriptive names (`cur_pos`, `facing_dir`, `cam_bounds`).
Stage camera bounds are accessed as `stage_info.cam_info.cam_bounds.left/right/top/bottom`, reusing the `StageBlastZone` field naming convention.
Temporary matching helpers use local inline names like `inlineE0`, `inlineM0`, `inlineD2`, and `inlineD3`; these are matching scaffolds rather than final semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `M2C_FIELD` for unresolved offsets inside hard functions, including fighter extension bytes/bitfields around `x1A88` and direct float reads at offsets like `0xB0`/`0xB4`.
Kept explicit labels and gotos such as `loop_24`, `block_18`, `block_40`, `block_41`, and `block_44` to preserve decompiled control flow shapes.
Left comments noting duplicate return nodes in `ftCo_800B0CA8`, indicating the implementation favors current match quality over readability.
Used `sqrtf`/`sqrtf__Ff` forms and squared-distance expressions to match floating-point codegen in proximity checks and nearest-ally selection logic.
Added `inlineM0(float x)` returning `127.0F * x` for nonnegative values and `128.0F * x` for negative values, matching analog stick conversion behavior in `ftCo_800B0918`.
Used `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)` in nearby matched code and retained low-level stack/control artifacts where necessary.
Updated a call from `ftCo_800B0CA8(fp0)` to `ftCo_800B0CA8(fp0, fp1)`, showing that signature recovery can require immediate caller repair for matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the title, match report, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t`, `x444`, or `x448`.
Search for later cleanup of `ftCo_800B0918`, especially the suspicious self-comparison/control-flow artifacts around `x444` and `x448`.
Search for later PRs that replace `M2C_FIELD` accesses in `ftCo_800AFC40`, `ftCo_800A50D4`, or `ftCo_800B0CA8` with typed `Fighter_x1A88_t` fields.
Check whether `ftCo_800A1CC4` later receives a semantic name or a fuller signature tied to stage blast-zone/camera-bound logic.
Search for uses of `StageCameraInfo.cam_bounds` to confirm the embedded `StageBlastZone` layout propagated cleanly across ground and stage code.
